visualization.title: Distribution of final grades across all courses

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT g.grade_value AS final_grade,
       COUNT(g.id) AS grade_count
FROM public.grades AS g
JOIN public.enrollments AS e ON e.id = g.enrollment_id
JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON cs.id = e.course_section_id
WHERE g.grade_value IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY g.grade_value
ORDER BY g.grade_value ASC;

issues: The x-encoding is set to type=nominal with sort="ascending" — that will perform lexicographic sorting in the visualization and may not match numeric ordering from the SQL if grade_value is numeric-like. Consider using a quantitative type for numeric grades or explicitly specifying the sort order based on grade_count or a numeric cast of final_grade.

metadata.sample shows only 2 of 6 rows — ensure the visualization data source includes all returned rows (rowCount=6) when rendering; this is a warning about the provided sample, not the SQL/viz logic.

visualization.title: Distribution of final grades across all courses

result.sql:
SELECT g.grade_value AS final_grade,
       COUNT(g.id) AS grade_count
FROM public.grades AS g
JOIN public.enrollments AS e ON e.id = g.enrollment_id
JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON cs.id = e.course_section_id
WHERE g.grade_value IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY g.grade_value
ORDER BY g.grade_value ASC;

issues: The x-encoding is set to type=nominal with sort="ascending" — that will perform lexicographic sorting in the visualization and may not match numeric ordering from the SQL if grade_value is numeric-like. Consider using a quantitative type for numeric grades or explicitly specifying the sort order based on grade_count or a numeric cast of final_grade.

metadata.sample shows only 2 of 6 rows — ensure the visualization data source includes all returned rows (rowCount=6) when rendering; this is a warning about the provided sample, not the SQL/viz logic.
